Step 1: Download your files This will download a .zip file, named yourName_WordGuessProject.zip with a folder of all the files you need for this project. It is recommended you save the zip file in the Documents directory. Right-click on .zip file to extract files. In the folder you are given a script.py file to write your code. Open this .py file in your desired python code editor. The words.txt file contains 5-letter words, with each word on a separate line. Only the words in this file are valid guesses for the user. It is important you do not move files out of this folder for your project to run. Download ZIP

The Project Run this code in your script file to simulate how your project will look like. Try different scenarios to get a good understanding of how the project runs. Step 3: Define get_started function This function stores name of the word file in a variable. Asks for user name. Prints: Welcome to Wordle 101 [userName] ======================================================================== Rules You have 6 guesses to figure out the solution. All solutions are words that are 5 letters long. Letters that have been guessed correctly are displayed in uppercase. Letters that are in the word but have been guessed in the wrong location are displayed in lowercase. ======================================================================== Returns file name.  Hint Use variables to store input from prompts input(). Copy and paste if needed. You can use \n in long sentences to continue a sentence on a new line. [userName] should be the name entered by the user.  More Information You can use f-string formatting to print a variable in a string. This works for any variable type (e.g. str, int).  Example print(f"Hello {variable_name}!") Step 5: Define get_random_word function This function takes a list words as an argument. The function picks a random position (index) in the list. Returns the word from the words list at that position.  Hint Use random.randrange() or random.randint() to get random position/number. Store this number in a variable.  More Information .randrange(start, stop) or (stop) generates a number from start or 0 (default) to stop - 1 (start number inclusive, stop number not inclusive). .randint(a, b) generates a number from a to b (both numbers inclusive). Use len(words) to get the total number on items in the list words. Make sure the index number exists in the words list (index starts at 0 to len(words)-1). To return a word from the list of words, think about indexing/how to access an item from a list.  Example list[index]  Test Code import urllib.request url = "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ed-tq/Python-Word-Guessing-Console-Game/refs/heads/main/05-test.py" with urllib.request.urlopen(url) as response: code = response.read().decode("utf-8") exec(code)

Step 6: Define get_player_guess function This function opens the file words.txt and reads all the words into a list called valid_words, where each word is stripped of whitespace and converted to lowercase. The function then repeatedly prompts the user to enter a guess using a while True loop. The user input should also be converted to lowercase.  The words from the file and the users input are converted to lowercase to ensure consistency when comparing values during validation. This prevents mismatches (e.g., HEllo vs hello), allowing valid words to be recognised regardless of how the user types them. The guess is validated using the following conditions: The guess must be exactly 5 letters long The guess must contain only alphabetic characters The guess must exist in the list of valid words If the guess does not meet any of the conditions, print an appropriate error message and the user is prompted again. Once a valid guess is entered, the function returns the guess in lowercase.  Hint You need to read words from a file called words.txt. Think about how to open a file safely in Python. Note: when you open a file you need to make sure to close it.  You can use a with open(...) statement so the file is automatically closed after reading. Once the file is open, you can loop through each line in the file to access the words.  Words may include extra whitespace or newline characters. Use strip() to clean each word Convert each word to lowercase using .lower() Store the cleaned words in a list using a list comprehension  You can chain methods/operations as long as each method/operation works on the object type returned by the previous one: "hello world".split()[0].upper(): .split() works on str, "hello world", returns a list ["hello", "world"]  [0] works on list, returns a str, "hello"  .upper() works on str, returns str Basic list comprehension format: new_list = [expression for item in iterable]  fruits = ["apple", "banana", "cherry", "kiwi", "mango"] upper_fruits = [] for f in fruits: upper_fruits.append(f.upper()) is the same as: upper_fruits = [f.upper() for f in fruits] To check if the number of letters is not 5  len(...) != 5 To check if the guess is not alphabetic  .isalpha() To check if the guess is not a valid word  not in  Test Code import urllib.request url = "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ed-tq/Python-Word-Guessing-Console-Game/refs/heads/main/06-test.py" with urllib.request.urlopen(url) as response: code = response.read().decode("utf-8") exec(code)

Step 7: Define play_round function This functions stimulates one round of the game. Takes an argument called word. Initialise guesses to 1 Create an initial game_state tuple of five underscores. Repeatedly ask the player for a guess by calling the function that gets player guess. Compare the guess to the target word. Do this by comparing each letter. Display each letter as: uppercase if in correct position lowercase if it is in the word but wrong position as an underscore if not in the word Stop if the user guesses the word correctly or after 6 guesses Return a tuple where: first argument is the number of guesses used second argument is True is the word was guesses, otherwise False  Hint You need a loop to allow the player up to 6 guesses. Start by setting guesses = 1. You can call get_player_guess() inside the loop to get each guess. Use range(len(word)) to loop through each position in the word.  If guess[i] == word[i], the letter is correct and in the correct position. If guess[i] in word, the letter is in the word but in the wrong position. Otherwise, display _.  Build the display string one character at a time. You may find it helpful to start with an empty display string such as display = "". Add a space after each symbol to match the required output format.  display = "" for i in range(len(word)): if guess[i] == word[i]: display += guess[i].upper() + " "  Test Code import urllib.request url = "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ed-tq/Python-Word-Guessing-Console-Game/refs/heads/main/07-test.py" with urllib.request.urlopen(url) as response: code = response.read().decode("utf-8") exec(code)

Step 8: Define play_more function This function runs one round of the game. This function takes two arguments read and rounds The function should call the appropriate function to create a list of valid words Call the appropriate function to choose one word from the list Print the round number as Round: X and a blank line after Call the appropriate function simulating a round Return a tuple where: first argument is the result returned by a round simulation second arguments is the chosen word  Hint Look back on previous functions and understand what they do. You can print the round number using an f-string. Step 9: Define play_game function This fucntions runs the full game of 1 or mroe rounds and shows summary This functions takes one argument filename Opens the file Reads the file Closes the file Initialises: rounds number of games number of wins a dictionary to track wins in across 1 to 6 guesses repeatedly call the appropriate function to play rounds after each round: check if player won print success or failure message showing the correct word update number of wins if player won ask the player if they want to continue Y to continue N to stop else print error message and ask again until valid If player stops playing: print summary showing win percentage and win distribution fucntion ends after printing the summary  Hint First open the file and read the words into a list. You can use .read().split() to turn the file contents into a list of words. Do not forget to close the file after reading.  You need counters to keep track of: the round number the total number of games played the total number of wins You also need a dictionary to count how many wins happened in 1 guess, 2 guesses, and so on up to 6 guesses. Use a while True: loop so the game keeps running until the player chooses to stop. At the start of each round, increase the round number by 1. Call play_more(read, rounds) to play one round.  The value returned by play_more(read, rounds) includes: the game result the chosen word You can use indexing to access them.  game = play_more(read, rounds) word = game[1] if the round was won update: number of wins the correct value inside dictionary of wins always increase number of games after each round  Ask the user if they want to continue by using input(). Keep asking until they enter either Y or N. If the user enters N, print the summary and stop the loop. The win percentage can be calculated using:  percentage = round(9number of wins / number of games) * 100) For the win distribution: if the count is 0, print the number otherwise print # repeated that many times, followed by count  Test Code import urllib.request url = "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ed-tq/Python-Word-Guessing-Console-Game/refs/heads/main/09-test.py" with urllib.request.urlopen(url) as response: code = response.read().decode("utf-8") exec(code)
